seoTitle: How to Add a Page in Confluence (2026)
metaDescription: Create a new Confluence page, apply a blueprint template, and configure access restrictions before publishing it live to your team.
intro: A messy or unstructured start to a new Confluence page can create headaches down the line. This guide covers creating a new page from your target space, applying a blueprint template if one fits, and setting access restrictions before you publish.

steps.heading: Choose your target space
steps.body: Move to your left sidebar, choose the space where the page belongs, and click the Create button at the top.

steps.heading: Or create from an existing page
steps.body: Hover over an existing page in the tree and click the contextual plus icon to create a child page in the same hierarchy.

steps.heading: Apply a blueprint template
steps.body: Open the right-hand drawer to search recommended blueprints, such as meeting trackers or project hubs, and insert one if it fits.

steps.heading: Title and structure your page
steps.body: Click the header container to input a clear, search-optimized title, then use inline formatting to set text layout and add labels.

steps.heading: Set access restrictions
steps.body: Click the lock icon to configure custom team access and view restrictions before wide distribution.

steps.heading: Publish the page
steps.body: Click the blue Publish button to push the finished content live to your shared index.

faq.question: Can I create a page as a child of an existing one?
faq.answer: Yes, hovering over an existing page in the sidebar tree and clicking the contextual plus icon creates a new page nested underneath it.

faq.question: Do I have to use a blueprint template?
faq.answer: No, blueprints are optional — you can start from a completely blank page if none of the templates fit your content.

faq.question: How do I restrict who can view a new page?
faq.answer: Click the lock icon before publishing to configure custom team access and view restriction rules.

faq.question: Can I add labels to a new page for easier searching?
faq.answer: Yes, you can attach searchable labels using the inline formatting tools before you publish.
